message:
refactor(vdbe): decompose write_page_internal into tiered concurrent write dispatch

The monolithic write_page_internal function handled three fundamentally
different write scenarios in a single deeply-nested control flow:
already-owned pages (fast re-write), first-touch pages (lock acquisition
with retry), and commit-surface-rare pages (page-one conflict tracking
with full MVCC machinery). This made the hot path carry dead code for
cold paths and vice versa.

Introduce ConcurrentWriteTier enum and classify_concurrent_write_tier()
to route writes to dedicated methods:

- Tier0AlreadyOwned: single lock, no retry loop, no snapshot check.
  Dominant path during balance/split read-modify-write cycles. Removes
  page-one conflict pre-tracking that was already handled at commit time,
  cutting handle lock acquisitions from 3 to 1.

- Tier1FirstTouch: lock acquisition + snapshot conflict check + retry
  loop, but no page-one conflict tracking (the pager now defers that
  for concurrent mode). Covers most ordinary data-page writes.

- Tier2CommitSurfaceRare: full page-one conflict tracking, commit-index
  check, and two-phase restore on failure. Used only for allocator
  metadata pages and non-concurrent fallback.

Also extract restore_concurrent_page_state() helper to eliminate
duplicated MVCC rollback logic across all three tiers.

Fix scalar function register slice bounds check: guard against negative
first_arg_reg (i32) before casting to usize, preventing potential
out-of-bounds access when the codegen emits negative register offsets.

Add test_shared_txn_page_io_concurrent_growth_write_does_not_block_on_page_one_pretracking
to verify tier classification for concurrent growth writes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
